variants.title: Dataset Open Question
variants.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, an ideal autotransformer is connected to a sinusoidal alternating current at the input. The output voltage can be adjusted by changing the position of the tap, which alters the number of turns at the output. A pilot light \( L \) is connected in parallel at the output, and a sliding rheostat \( R \) is connected in the output circuit. The position of the slider can change the resistance value of \( R \). Both meters are ideal AC meters. When switch \( S \) is closed, how does sliding the tap downward affect the input power?
variants.expected_answer: When switch \( S \) is closed, sliding the tap downward decreases the input power

variants.title: Magnetic Field Inside the Hollow Region
variants.rewritten_question_text: An infinitely long hollow cylindrical conductor has inner radius \( a \) and outer radius \( b \). Its current is uniformly distributed over the conducting cross-section. What is the magnitude of the magnetic field \( B \) at distances \( r<a \) from the center axis?

variants.title: Magnetic Field Within the Conducting Shell
variants.rewritten_question_text: An infinitely long hollow cylindrical conductor has inner radius \( a \) and outer radius \( b \). Its current is uniformly distributed over the conducting cross-section. As the distance \( r \) increases through the conducting region \( a<r<b \), how does the magnitude of the magnetic field \( B \) change qualitatively?
variants.expected_answer: It increases with \( r \), rising from zero at \( r=a \) to its maximum at \( r=b \).

variants.title: Magnetic Field Outside the Conductor
variants.rewritten_question_text: An infinitely long hollow cylindrical conductor has inner radius \( a \) and outer radius \( b \). Its current is uniformly distributed over the conducting cross-section. For points outside the conductor, \( r>b \), how does the magnitude of the magnetic field \( B \) depend on \( r \)?
variants.expected_answer: It decreases inversely with distance, i.e. \( B \propto 1/r \).

rationale: Question is already open-ended.
